channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Power restored after outage across several Brussels municipalities
item.description: The network operator for the distribution of natural gas and electricity in Brussels, Sibelga, reported outages in three municipalities.

item.title: Up to 32°C in Brussels: Is Belgium heading toward first heatwave of 2026?
item.description: Belgium has been plagued by rain, hail and thunderstorms in recent months, but the country has been experiencing exactly the opposite the past couple of days.

item.title: 'I saw you, get lost': Women confront man masturbating in Brussels park
item.description: Several women confronted a man in Forest Park on Friday, who they claim was pleasuring himself in front of them.

item.title: ‘Brussels is beautiful – let’s show it’: Region begins removing thousands of concrete blocks
item.description: The operation marks the beginning of a plan to progressively dismantle many of the approximately 3,000 concrete blocks currently spread across the Brussels-Capital Region.

item.title: Why these Brussels residents choose green spaces over social housing
item.description: Angry residents want to stop the construction of around 70 social housing units and additional middle-income housing on land currently used by an urban farm.

item.title: 'Speculative bubble': Ixelles draws a line on student housing
item.description: With nearly half of Brussels’ students based there, Ixelles has long branded itself as the student municipality par excellence – but this may soon all change.

item.title: Council calls for ban on night flights in Brussels amid 'unbearable' noise for residents
item.description: Residents in some areas are faced with aircraft passing overhead as frequently as every four minutes, totalling over 200 movements some days.

item.title: Severely injured man found on street in Brussels
item.description: Brussels police establish a security zone after a man was found stabbed near a police station entrance. Authorities confirm a suspect is in custody and the inquiry is ongoing.

item.title: Brussels shooters flee on scooters
item.description: A quiet Brussels street becomes the scene of gunfire; police investigate as suspects escape. No comment yet on injuries or arrests.

item.title: 30 passengers 'feeling unwell' evacuated from Brussels metro station
item.description: Emergency crews respond to Brussels metro evacuation. Pepper spray suspected after passengers report breathing difficulties; two hospitalised in serious incident.

item.title: In Brussels, single mothers struggle to remain in the job market
item.description: In Brussels, one in three households with children (33.7%) are single-parent families.

item.title: Zombieing: The new dating trend no one asked for
item.description: I was in my Bridget Jones era and feeling unusually satisfied with singlehood. But then I got an unexpected text.

item.title: Agreement in place to represent all sensitivities of Islam in worship
item.description: A landmark meeting in Brussels brings hope for unity as Belgium’s Islamic communities move to end years of organisational turmoil.

item.title: Ixelles cracks down on self-service fast-food joints by raising taxes
item.description: "We consider that within a commercial fabric, it diminishes the more convivial aspect," an Ixelles councillor told The Brussels Times.

item.title: New edition of Brussels zine celebrates Wiels Quarter and its 'Unionistes' fan bars
item.description: "This is what the Pintjes zine is all about: landing in an area of the city you don’t know, wanting to go for a beer but not knowing where to start."

item.title: Air pollution in Brussels: Call for 'green school streets' gets louder as harmful fumes persist
item.description: More than 120 schools in the Brussels-Capital Region are exposed to pollution levels between two and three times above the WHO threshold.

item.title: 'Brussels is afraid to be ambitious': Kanal museum’s quitting director has no regrets
item.description: "It’s not easy to fulfil such a project of this scale in Brussels. It will be the biggest museum to open in Brussels in more than a century."

item.title: 'We want the city centre to be spotless': Steam-cleaning operation launched to clean Brussels street furniture
item.description: Benches, traffic signs, smart bins, public toilets, lighting poles, fountains, bicycle racks and kiosks are all being cleaned with superheated water and steam.

item.title: Summer Dance Festival returns to Bozar
item.description: Around 15 artists will perform in the Henry le Bœuf Hall, including Sangeun Lee and Gareth Haw, principal dancers of the English National Ballet.

item.title: Francophone teachers protest austerity plans in Brussels
item.description: Hundreds of French-speaking educators took to the streets on Friday to send a clear message to the government as budget cuts loom for schools.
